summary: Implemented first-class persisted GateResult evidence through canonical aletheia_audit_events actions rather than a new gate snapshot table. Final memo export now requires an approved checkpoint plus a server-persisted passing gate snapshot and gate authorization audit event before the final work product/export audit event is written.
contractsChanged: Local/Supabase createWorkProduct(final_memo) requires persisted passing gate snapshot evidence
Final memo content persistedGateEvidence includes gateSnapshotAuditEventId and gateAuthorizationAuditEventId
final_memo_exported audit details include approvalCheckpointId, gateSnapshotAuditEventId, and gateAuthorizationAuditEventId
POST /aletheia/matters/:matterId/gate-snapshots route persists canonical gate snapshot audit events
Audit integrity checks verify final memo exports resolve to passing gate snapshots and gate authorization events
risks: No migration-backed gate snapshot table was added; the canonical persistence source is aletheia_audit_events details with schemaVersion aletheia-gate-snapshot-v0.

GateResult payloads are still supplied by the existing deterministic frontend gate engine, but final authorization now uses the server-written audit event IDs generated during createWorkProduct(final_memo).
The broader worktree contains many unrelated parallel uncommitted changes from other owners; this lane did not revert them.
Full frontend UI smoke was not rerun in this lane because no frontend UI files were changed.
needs: Gate Engine owner can now remove the backend/audit persistence blocker and treat persisted gate snapshot evidence as the final memo authorization contract.
Future hardening can add database constraints or a dedicated gate snapshot table if audit volume/querying requires it, but it is not required for the current first-class persistence closure.
